bootstrap.note: Branded-CDN quick-try: auto-installs the polyfill. Module scripts are deferred, so navigator.bluetooth mounts after the document parses (the click-triggered requestDevice below runs well after that, so it's fine). For apps that read navigator.bluetooth at parse time, prefer the npm/bundled path: npm install @beacio/core, then import '@beacio/core/auto' as the first import.

steps: Install the beacio Safari Web Extension from the App Store on the target iPhone and enable it under Settings → Apps → Safari → Extensions → beacio with 'Always Allow on Every Website' granted.

Drop a single <script type="module">import 'https://cdn.beacio.com/@beacio/core@1.0.0/dist/auto.mjs';</script> tag into <head> — it auto-installs the polyfill from the branded CDN, no build step required. (For a bundled app, instead run `npm install @beacio/core` and `import '@beacio/core/auto'` as the first import — required if you read navigator.bluetooth at parse time, since module scripts are deferred.)
Invoke navigator.bluetooth.requestDevice() from a user gesture (click/tap). Values stream via characteristicvaluechanged events once notifications are started.
Serve the page over HTTPS — Web Bluetooth requires a secure context.
Install beacio Safari Web Extension on the iPhone and enable it on every website.
Install the polyfill and React bindings:  npm install @beacio/core @beacio/react
Import '@beacio/core/auto' once at the entry (main.tsx) so navigator.bluetooth is installed before any component renders.
Use the useConnection hook to drive a click-triggered requestDevice + GATT connect and stream characteristic values into component state.
Install beacio Safari Web Extension on the iPhone and enable it on every website.
Install the polyfill: npm install @beacio/core
Import '@beacio/core/auto' once in main.ts before createApp() so navigator.bluetooth mounts before any component renders.
Invoke navigator.bluetooth.requestDevice() from a @click handler — it must run under user activation.
Install beacio Safari Web Extension on the iPhone and enable it on every website.
Install the polyfill: npm install @beacio/core
Import '@beacio/core/auto' once in src/main.ts before mounting the root component so navigator.bluetooth is present on first render.
Call navigator.bluetooth.requestDevice() from an on:click handler to satisfy the user-activation requirement.
Install beacio Safari Web Extension on the iPhone and enable it on every website.
Install the polyfill and W3C types:  npm install @beacio/core  && npm install --save-dev @types/web-bluetooth
Import '@beacio/core/auto' once in src/main.ts before bootstrapApplication() so navigator.bluetooth mounts before any zone/component runs.
Invoke navigator.bluetooth.requestDevice() from a (click) handler; run value updates through NgZone if you touch change detection.

bootstrap.note: Client-only: wrap your root layout's children with <BeacioProvider>. Never import the polyfill at module scope under SSR.

steps: Install beacio Safari Web Extension on the iPhone and enable it on every website.
Install the polyfill and React bindings:  npm install @beacio/core @beacio/react
Gate the polyfill to the browser by dynamic-importing '@beacio/core/auto' inside a 'use client' boundary (never at module scope — it would run during SSR).
Drive requestDevice() from a click handler in a client component; stream characteristic values into React state.
